mnemonics: Initial Parameter Estimates Can Be Made Easily: Use Sample Mean, Variance, and Autocorrelation (IPECBME)
keyPoints: For an AR(1) model, the initial estimate for the mean (μ) is the sample mean of the time series.
For an AR(1) model, the initial estimate for the autoregressive parameter (φ₁) is the sample autocorrelation at lag 1.
The initial estimate for the white noise variance (σ²) is derived from the sample variance and the estimated φ₁ using the formula: σ²_initial = γ₀(1 - φ₁²), where γ₀ is the sample variance.
commonMistakes: Using the sample variance directly as the estimate for the white noise variance (σ²) without adjusting for the autoregressive structure.
Forgetting to center the data by subtracting the sample mean before estimating the autocorrelations for the autoregressive parameters.
Applying these simple initial estimation methods designed for basic AR(1) models to more complex models like ARMA(p,q) or ARIMA(p,d,q) without appropriate modifications.
